findings.description: The DRAFT watermark visible in the PDF is absent in the HTML version
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows a large diagonal 'DRAFT' watermark across the page; HTML has no watermark

findings.description: HydroLogics logo appears smaller/differently styled in HTML but is present
findings.evidence: PDF has logo centered at bottom; HTML has it similarly placed but sizing differs slightly

findings.description: Table of Contents section heading underline formatting differs
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'Section' and 'Page' with underline formatting; HTML uses a table structure instead

findings.description: Table of Contents rendered as a proper table in HTML vs. spaced text in PDF
findings.evidence: PDF uses tab-stop aligned text for TOC; HTML uses a bordered table with cells - same data preserved

findings.description: Appendices section formatting differs slightly between PDF and HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows appendices as indented list under TOC; HTML shows them as separate paragraphs outside the table

findings.description: DRAFT watermark absent from flow chart pages in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF pages 10, 11, 12, 14, 15, 16 have DRAFT watermarks; HTML versions do not

findings.description: Flow chart diagrams are missing from HTML conversion - rendered as text-only lists
findings.evidence: PDF page 10 shows a detailed flow chart diagram with Reservoir Nodes (red triangles), Demand Nodes (blue squares), Junction Nodes (yellow circles), and connecting arcs for Upper Cape Fear Basin. HTML page shows only text labels like 'Reidsville Dam', 'Lake Brandt', 'Greensboro Demand' etc. as a plain text list without the diagram

findings.description: Flow chart diagram for Middle Cape Fear Basin is missing from HTML
findings.evidence: PDF page 11 shows detailed flow chart with nodes and arcs; HTML shows only text labels as a list

findings.description: Flow chart diagram for Deep River is missing from HTML
findings.evidence: PDF page 12 shows detailed flow chart with reservoir/demand/junction nodes; HTML shows only text labels

findings.description: Flow chart diagram for Lower Cape Fear Basin is missing from HTML
findings.evidence: PDF page 13 shows flow chart with nodes, arcs, and legend; HTML shows only text labels as a list

findings.description: Flow chart diagram for Upper Neuse Basin is missing from HTML
findings.evidence: PDF page 14 shows detailed flow chart; HTML shows only text labels and annotations as plain text

findings.description: Flow chart diagram for Middle Neuse Basin is missing from HTML
findings.evidence: PDF page 15 shows flow chart with nodes and connecting arcs; HTML shows only text labels

findings.description: Flow chart diagram for Lower Neuse Basin is missing from HTML
findings.evidence: PDF page 16 shows flow chart with Buckhorn Reservoir, Wilson Demand, etc.; HTML shows only text labels

findings.description: Flow chart legend information rendered as plain text list items instead of a visual legend
findings.evidence: PDF shows colored shape legends (red triangle = Reservoir Node, blue square = Demand Node, etc.); HTML shows these as simple text lines

findings.description: Map/schematic image of the basin model is present in HTML but may have slight sizing differences
findings.evidence: PDF page 6 shows the full basin schematic map; HTML appears to show the same map image

findings.description: Section headings 'Static and Pattern Data', 'Timeseries Data', 'Operating Rules' formatting differs
findings.evidence: PDF shows these as bold underlined subheadings; HTML shows them as bold text without underline

findings.description: Flow chart titles rendered as h2 headings break the document hierarchy. These are subsection content within Section 2.2 Model Input and should not be at the same level as 'Section 2. Model Components'. The PDF presents them as figure/chart titles, not section headings.
findings.evidence: HTML uses <h2> for 'Flow Chart of Major Nodes in the Upper Cape Fear Basin', 'Flow Chart of Major Nodes in the Middle Cape Fear Basin', etc. These are chart titles within section 2.2, not peer-level sections to 'Section 2. Model Components'.

findings.description: Multiple h1 tags effectively exist. The title uses h1, but 'Section 1. Introduction' and 'Section 2. Model Components' are rendered as h2, which is acceptable. However, the flow chart titles being h2 alongside these creates a confusing flat hierarchy.
findings.evidence: Six flow chart titles at h2 level alongside two main section headings at h2 level.

findings.description: The Table of Contents is rendered as a table but the subsection entries (2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.4) lack indentation styling that the PDF shows, and '2 – Model Components' row has no page number which matches the PDF but the row appears as a peer to '1 – Introduction' without visual hierarchy.
findings.evidence: PDF shows indented subsections 2.1-2.4 under '2 – Model Components'; HTML table has them as flat rows.

findings.description: An incomplete sentence structure exists in the PDF source regarding the 58 reservoir count, and the HTML faithfully reproduces this awkward text. This is not an error in conversion.
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML: 'There are 58 reservoir 42 actual reservoirs, 11 flood impoundments on Crabtree Creek, and five artificial storage nodes used for time-of-travel flow routing)' - missing opening parenthesis present in both.

findings.description: The flow chart diagrams on pages 10-16 are complex visual diagrams with annotations. The HTML attempts to capture the text content from these diagrams as paragraphs, but the spatial relationships, legend symbols, and flow connections inherent in the visual diagrams are lost.
findings.evidence: Pages 10-16 contain flowchart diagrams with nodes, arcs, and annotations. HTML renders these as sequential paragraph text, losing the diagrammatic structure.
